codeReview.summary: Reviewed handoff, transcript skeleton, and commit eee025d. The implementation wires exec/default routing through `dispatch_exec`, correctly dispatches `exec clone`, `exec worktree`, and `exec cd`, preserves URL shorthand to clone for both `exec <url>` and `exec cd <url>`, and routes unknown/no-subcommand exec paths to the cd selector stub. The cd path loads entries, supports deterministic `--and-keys`/`--and-exit` behavior, and emits shell scripts through existing script builders; no actionable correctness or regression issues were identified against this feature’s requirements.

sharedStateObservations.observation: Shared guidance appears stale relative to the implementation pattern used by this and prior features: docs prescribe clap/ratatui-driven flows while current code uses manual CLI parsing and command-local injected key handling.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: AGENTS.md:14-15 says to use `clap` derive macros and ratatui/crossterm TUI flow; architecture.md:16,43,51,93 documents clap parsing and `src/tui/test_keys.rs` injection path. Current implementation uses manual parsing in src/cli.rs:152-158 and in-feature key parsing/handling in src/commands/cd.rs:191 and 519-641.

summary: Pass. Exec routing integration is correctly implemented for clone/worktree/cd/default and URL shorthand, with no actionable defects found for this feature scope.
